questions.text: The Sun Temple at Konark was built by which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Sun Temple at Konark (Odisha) was built by King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga dynasty in the 13th century CE. It is designed as a giant chariot with 24 wheels for the Sun God. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site (1984).

questions.text: The Brihadeeswarar Temple in Thanjavur was built by which Chola ruler?
questions.explanation: The Brihadeeswarar Temple (also called Big Temple) in Thanjavur was built by Raja Raja Chola I, completed in 1010 CE. At 66 metres, its vimana (tower) is the tallest in India. It is part of the UNESCO 'Great Living Chola Temples' designation.

questions.text: The Khajuraho group of monuments is associated with which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Khajuraho temples in Madhya Pradesh were built primarily by the Chandela dynasty (10th–11th century CE). The temples are renowned for their intricate erotic sculptures, though these represent only about 10% of the total carvings. They are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.text: Which architectural style has the distinctive feature of a 'Gopuram' — a massive entrance gateway tower?
questions.explanation: Gopuram (monumental entrance gateway towers) is the characteristic feature of Dravida (South Indian) temple architecture. In large South Indian temple complexes, the Gopurams often surpass the main Vimana in height. The Meenakshi Temple in Madurai has 14 gopurams.

questions.text: Hoysala temples like those at Belur and Halebidu are examples of which architectural style?
questions.explanation: Hoysala temples (12th–13th century, Karnataka) are the classic examples of Vesara style — a mixture of Nagara and Dravida. They have no tall shikhara or tall gopuram, but feature elaborate star-shaped (stellate) plans, intricate sculptural friezes, and are built in soft chloritic schite (soapstone).

questions.text: What is the 'Amalaka' in Nagara temple architecture?
options: The ribbed disc-shaped stone crowning the Nagara shikhara before the finial
The circumambulatory passage around the sanctum
questions.explanation: Amalaka is the characteristic ribbed, disc-shaped stone element placed just below the Kalasha (metal pot finial) at the top of a Nagara shikhara. It is one of the most distinctive diagnostic features of Nagara style temples. The word 'amalaka' comes from the Indian gooseberry fruit it resembles.

questions.text: The Shore Temple at Mahabalipuram belongs to which dynasty and is significant as what?
options: Pallava dynasty; oldest surviving stone structural temple in South India
Chalukya dynasty; example of Vesara style
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple at Mahabalipuram (Tamil Nadu) was built by the Pallava dynasty (7th–8th century CE) and is considered the oldest surviving free-standing stone structural (not rock-cut) temple in South India. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site (1984, as part of Mahabalipuram monuments).

questions.text: Aihole in Karnataka is called the 'Cradle of Indian Architecture'. Why?
options: It has the oldest surviving temple in India
Chalukyan architects experimented with different temple styles here, making it a laboratory of Indian architecture
It was the first place Mughal architecture mixed with Indian style
It contains the most temples per square kilometre in any Indian city
questions.explanation: Aihole (Chalukyan period, 6th–8th century CE) is called the 'Cradle of Indian Architecture' because the Chalukyan architects used it as an experimental site. Over 125 temples of varying plans and styles exist here — some Nagara, some Dravida, some mixed — showing the evolution of Indian temple architecture.

questions.text: What is the largest functioning Hindu temple complex in the world?
questions.explanation: The Srirangam Ranganathaswamy Temple in Trichy, Tamil Nadu is considered the largest functioning Hindu temple complex in the world, covering about 156 acres with 7 concentric prakaras (enclosure walls). It has 21 gopurams, with the main Rajagopuram reaching 73 metres.

questions.text: The Dilwara Jain temples at Mount Abu are famous for being built primarily from which material?
questions.explanation: The Dilwara Jain temples (11th–13th century) at Mount Abu in Rajasthan are built from pure white marble. They are exceptional for their intricate carved ceilings and pillars, with virtually no decoration on the exterior — all beauty is concentrated inside. Vimal Vasahi (1031 CE) is the oldest.

questions.text: Pattadakal in Karnataka (UNESCO World Heritage Site) is historically important because:
options: It has the oldest Jain temple in South India
It contains temples of both Nagara and Dravida styles side by side
It was the capital of Vijayanagara Empire
It has the largest collection of rock-cut caves in Deccan
questions.explanation: Pattadakal (Chalukya period, 7th–8th century) is a unique UNESCO WHS in Karnataka because it contains temples of both Nagara and Dravida styles constructed simultaneously by Chalukyan rulers. This makes it a living record of how both great traditions of Indian temple architecture coexisted and were practiced side-by-side.

questions.text: Which South Indian temple is famous for having the longest corridor among any temple in India?
questions.explanation: The Ramanathaswamy Temple (Rameshwaram) in Tamil Nadu has the longest corridor (~1,200 metres/1.2 km) of any temple in India, flanked by thousands of carved pillars. It is one of the four Dhams and is in the Dravida architectural tradition with massive prakaras.

questions.text: The Lingaraj Temple in Bhubaneswar represents which style of temple architecture?
questions.explanation: The Lingaraj Temple (11th century) in Bhubaneswar, Odisha is a magnificent example of Nagara/Kalinga/Deula style — a variant of Nagara style characteristic of Odisha. It has a curved Rekha shikhara (tower) and is the largest temple in Bhubaneswar, dedicated to Shiva as Harihara.

questions.text: In Dravida temple architecture, what is the term for the main tower above the sanctum?
questions.explanation: In Dravida (South Indian) architecture, the tower above the sanctum (garbhagriha) is called the Vimana — a stepped pyramid with multiple tiers. In Nagara (North Indian) architecture, the equivalent is called Shikhara. Gopuram is the monumental gateway tower at the outer boundaries of South Indian temple complexes.

questions.text: The Hoysala temple complex at which location is known for the best-preserved Hoysala temple with a trikuta (triple-shrine) plan?
questions.explanation: The Kesava Temple at Somanathapur (Mysuru district, Karnataka) is the best-preserved Hoysala temple and uniquely has a trikuta (three-shrine) layout on a single star-shaped platform. It was built in 1268 CE. Belur has the Chennakesava Temple; Halebidu has the twin Hoysaleswara Temple.

questions.text: Which feature is most diagnostic of Nagara temple architecture?
options: A flat pyramidal tower with multiple tiers (vimana)
A curvilinear tapering tower (shikhara) above the sanctum
Multiple massive gateway towers (gopurams) at the boundary walls
Horizontal layered construction with sloping battered walls
questions.explanation: The curvilinear tapering shikhara above the garbhagriha (sanctum) is the most diagnostic feature of Nagara temple architecture. This distinguishes it from Dravida style (stepped/pyramidal vimana) and from Hoysala/Vesara style (no prominent tower). The shikhara symbolises Mount Meru, axis of the universe.
